understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to place at least one selectable piece onto every goal tile (+) in each level simultaneously, thereby achieving a WIN state for the level and ultimately winning the whole game. Multiple selectable pieces could exist, and sometimes you needed to use CLICK to switch between them to move each onto a separate goal.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was performed using MOVE actions in four directions. Pushing occurred when a pushable object (block) was immediately adjacent in the movement direction, causing it to shift along with the selected piece. Walls blocked movement, but some boundaries behaved differently. Switching control between multiple selectable pieces was done via CLICK with target_position using the same pixel coordinate system as player.position. The semantic_grid showed P for the currently selected piece, S for other selectables, B for blocks, # for walls, + for goals, and . for empty spaces.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to place at least one selectable piece onto every goal tile (+) in each level simultaneously, thereby achieving a WIN state for the level and ultimately winning the whole game. Multiple selectable pieces could exist, and sometimes you needed to use CLICK to switch between them to move each onto a separate goal.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was performed using MOVE actions in four directions. Pushing occurred when a pushable object (block) was immediately adjacent in the movement direction, causing it to shift along with the selected piece. Walls blocked movement, but some boundaries behaved differently. Switching control between multiple selectable pieces was done via CLICK with target_position using the same pixel coordinate system as player.position. The semantic_grid showed P for the currently selected piece, S for other selectables, B for blocks, # for walls, + for goals, and . for empty spaces.
